title: brickellia longifolia
intro: Brickellia longifolia, also known as Mountain Sage, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the aster family. Its leaves, stems, and flowers are used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Brickellia longifolia include reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of colds, flu, and allergies, as well as soothing digestive issues and promoting relaxation. Brickellia longifolia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies for its expectorant and antispasmodic properties.
